subject: English Language
q:
The following question is based on Khadija Abububakar Jalili's The Life Changer.

The character referred to as the quiet one in the novel was named Talle because
options: no one really knew who fathered him
his mother died shortly after he was born
he was a reticent person while growing up
he was a product of traditional medicine
explanation: "The quiet one" was named Talle on account of the fact that his mother died shortly after his birth. This can be found on page 21 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q:
The following question is based on Khadija Abububakar Jalili's The Life Changer.

What is the status of Habib Lawal in the House of Assembly
explanation: Habib Lawal's title in the House of Assembly is "Speaker". This can be found on page 58 of the novel where Salma examines his office as she walks into it and reads the name on his desk: "HABIB LAWAL, SPEAKER".

subject: English Language
q:
The following question is based on Khadija Abububakar Jalili's The Life Changer.

At first, Salma did not like the
options: ethnic groups where her roommates came from
composition of her roommates in the hostel
room she was allocated in Queen Amina Hall
the hostel she was allocated to in Queen Amina Hall
explanation: Salma, at first, did not like the composition of her roommates. Her roommates: Ngozi, Tomiwa and Ada all came from different parts of the country. She actually liked the "most coveted"  and "famous" Queen Amina Hall but did not take a liking to her roommates. This can be found on page 31 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q:
The following question is based on Khadija Abububakar Jalili's The Life Changer.

The use of the expression Notes of Salvation is
explanation: Notes of Salvation is a euphemism for the cheat notes that Kola passed to Salma during the examination. It helps to reduce the unpleasantness of what the cheat notes represent.

subject: English Language
q:
The following question is based on Khadija Abububakar Jalili's The Life Changer.

The acronym "EMEC" as used in the novel means
explanation: The acronym "EMEC" means "Examination Malpractice and Ethics Committee". We can find this on page 59 of the novel. It is in front of this committee that Selma and Kola had to appear for the Committee to decide their punishment for committing examination malpractice.

subject: English Language
q:
The following question is based on Khadija Abububakar Jalili's The Life Changer.

How much did Dr Kabir Mohammed collect from Salma in order to cover up her EMAL offence?
options: one hundred and fifty thousand naira
explanation: Dr Kabir Muhammed collected one hundred thousand Naira from Salma to cover up her EMAL offence. This is after she negotiated it down from two hundred thousand Naira. This can be found on page 61 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q:
The following question is based on Khadija Abububakar Jalili's The Life Changer.

According to the novel, Bint's Social Studies teacher is
explanation: Bint describes her Social Studies teacher as meddlesome as she narrates how she took on her Social Studies teacher who thinks he knows "a little bit about every subject under the sun". This can be found on page 1 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: What option best fills the gap labelled 6
explanation: In mining, "tunnels" are pathways that connect different parts of a mine.

subject: English Language
q: What option best fills the gap labelled 7
explanation: The people working in the mines are specifically referred to as "miners," not "toilers" or "workers."

subject: English Language
q: What option best fills the gap labelled 8
explanation: The "coal face" refers to the exposed surface of coal where mining takes place.

subject: English Language
q: What option best fills the gap labelled 9
explanation: "Bare" describes the exposed or uncovered coal surface. Other options do not fit the description appropriately.

subject: English Language
q: What option best fills the gap labelled 10
explanation: The "beams" are bars that support the span of the roof between two supports, props or walls.

subject: English Language
q: What option best fills the gap labelled 11
explanation: "Props" are temporary supports used in mines to prevent collapses.

subject: English Language
q: What option best fills the gap labelled 12
explanation: A "hole" is what the miners crawl through to get to the coal.

subject: English Language
q: What option best fills the gap labelled 13?
explanation: The combination of "digging" and explosives is what is needed to harvest coal from a mine.

subject: English Language
q: What option best fills the gap labelled 14?
explanation: A mechanical "knife" is used to cut coal to separate it from the base.

subject: English Language
q: What option best fills the gap labelled 15?
explanation: Explosives are "detonated" (set off).

subject: English Language
q: From the passage, it can be inferred that
options: Climate and weathering are not related
Climate determines the type of weathering
Weathering can only occur where the temperature is cool and water is available
Weathering is possible only in a place where the temperature is hot
explanation: The passage explains that the type of weathering depends on climatic conditions: physical or mechanical weathering occurs with temperature and water, while chemical weathering thrives in hot climates.

subject: English Language
q: A suitable title for the passage above is
explanation: The passage focuses on the process of weathering, its types (physical/mechanical and chemical), and the factors that influence it, particularly climatic conditions. Therefore, "Weathering" is the most appropriate title.

subject: English Language
q: The writer was puzzled because
options: he saw big trees growing from the rocks
he remembered what he learnt in school
he was where the atmosphere was cool
he was left alone wandering in a desert
explanation: The passage states, "I noticed big trees with their roots penetrating into rocks and breaking them. I initially wondered and puzzled seeing trees growing from the rock." This clearly indicates that the writer was puzzled by the sight of trees growing from the rocks.

subject: English Language
q: Learning in the four walls of a classroom implies
explanation: Learning within the "four walls of a classroom" refers to formal education, which is structured and organised and takes place in a school.

subject: English Language
q: One can conclude from the passage that
options: rock particles cannot be found in the soil
rock particles can be found in the clay soil
rock particles can only be found in sandy soil
rock particles can be found in any soil
explanation: he passage mentions that the writer observed rock particles mixing with the rest of the soil during soil sampling. This implies that rock particles are not exclusive to any specific type of soil; they can be part of soil in general.

subject: English Language
q:
In the following question, select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

He was building a castle in the air.
options: He was imagining a terrible future
He was imagining a possible future as a famous architect
He was imagining a wonderful and rather impossible future
explanation: The phrase "building a castle in the air" is an idiom that refers to imagining or planning something fanciful, unrealistic or highly unlikely to happen.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

Jude appears to sometimes enjoy playing the devil's advocate.
options: Jude seems to enjoy acting as the devil in films
Jude seems to enjoy playing the game
Jude seems to enjoy advancing the unpopular side of an argument
Jude seems to always enjoy appearing differently
explanation: The phrase "playing the devil's advocate" is an idiomatic expression that means arguing for a position one may not necessarily believe in to provoke debate or test the strength of an opposing argument.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

Yerima always holds his own in any situation he finds himself.
explanation: The phrase "holds his own in any situation" means that Yerima is capable of maintaining his position, handling himself well, or standing firm regardless of the circumstances. This means that he has confidence and composure.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

Titi was the cynosure of all eyes at the party.
options: She was the centre of attraction
She was eager to leave the party
explanation: The phrase "cynosure of all eyes" means someone who is the focus of attention or admiration. In this context, it suggests that Titi drew everyone's attention at the party.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

Let this case stand over for the present, the lawyer pleaded.
options: The case should be discussed immediately
The case should be struck out
The case is pending in court
explanation: The phrase "stand over for the present" means to temporarily delay or postpone something to a later time. It is used in legal or formal contexts to request that a matter be deferred.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The judge declared the two accused guilty.
explanation: The phrase "declared the two accused guilty" means that the judge found the two accused individuals responsible for the crime. The opposite of guilty is innocent.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

A prosperous lawyer is definitely hardworking.
explanation: The word "prosperous" means successful or thriving in terms of wealth or financial stability. The opposite of prosperous is poor.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

Fake news is rife on social media platforms.
explanation: In this context, "fake news" refers to misleading or false information. The opposite of fake news would be authentic news, meaning real or genuine.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The children's room was messy when we entered.
explanation: The word "messy" means disorganized or untidy. The opposite of messy is tidy, which means clean and organized.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The judge pronounced the suspect guilty.
explanation: The word "guilty" means found responsible for a crime. The opposite of guilty is acquitted, which means to be declared not guilty of a crime.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The lecturer's wife has become quite unconscionable.
explanation: The word "unconscionable" refers to behaviour that is immoral or unethical and beyond what is reasonable or just. The nearest in meaning to the word unconscionable is unbecoming, which means inappropriate or not in keeping with the standards of behaviour expected in a particular context, though less severe than unconscionable.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

He bequeathed his company to his last daughter.
explanation: The word "bequeathed" means to leave something (especially property or possessions) to someone in a will. The nearest meaning to bequeathed is left, as in leaving something behind for someone.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

She went to the court to seek redress for the wrong done to her.
explanation: The word "redress" means seeking justice or compensation for a wrong or injury. In this context, redress means asking for compensation to make up for the harm or injustice done.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The attempt by the robbers to break into the bank was futile.
explanation: The word "futile" means something that is pointless or without success. The nearest meaning to futile is fruitless, which also means producing no results or being unsuccessful.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The newly employed teachers gave their work rapt attention.
explanation: Paying "rapt" attention means paying full attention or being fully absorbed in something. The nearest meaning to rapt attention is concentrated, which refers to focusing all one's attention on a task or activity.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Sally assisted me in buying my _____ yesterday.
explanation: The phrase "very first party dress" is the grammatically correct option because the word "very" is used to emphasise the first party dress, which is the key element in the sentence.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

You know that you ____ it before tomorrow.
explanation: The phrase "ought to do" means that there is an expectation or obligation to complete something by tomorrow.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Maryam, the girl in my neighbourhood, treats people as if she _____ better than they are.
explanation: The sentence uses a hypothetical situation (Maryam treating people as if she is better than they are), and in such cases, we use the subjunctive mood, which requires the verb "were" instead of "is" or "was" when expressing unreal or hypothetical conditions.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

But for the timely intervention of the police, the thief ______ by the angry mob.
explanation: The phrase "But for the timely intervention of the police" indicates a hypothetical situation in the past, which suggests that the thief would have been lynched if the police hadn't intervened. In such a situation, the correct form to use is the past perfect conditional, "would have been lynched."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

My father loves bringing ___ old memories.
explanation: The phrase "bringing up old memories" is an idiomatic expression that means to mention or recall past memories.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Women always ______ themselves in beautiful apparel.
explanation: The word "adorn" means to decorate or add beauty to oneself or something.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

The movie wasn't fantastic, was it? _____
explanation: The appropriate answer to this is "No, it wasn't" to show agreement with the speaker. Question tags are used to elicit agreement from a listener and therefore the most appropriate response should be one of agreement.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

My uncle is not sure ____ the strange guests were.
explanation: The sentence refers to people, specifically the "strange guests," so the correct word to use is "who", which is used to refer to people.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Everyone in class wanted the Literature teacher to ______ the point of view of the _______.
explanation: "Expatiate" means to elaborate or explain something in detail. "Playwright" is someone who writes plays. Other options have wrong spellings of the words.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

____ of his lateness, Okupe gained from the coaching.
explanation: "In spite of" is used to show contrast with something (in this case, Okupe's lateness). The phrase means that despite being late, Okupe still benefited from the coaching.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

The man is not as powerful as he once was, his influence has considerably _______
explanation: "Diminished" means reduced in size, strength or importance. It shows that the man's influence has lessened over time.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

I can't lend him my books. I ______ know him.
explanation: "Scarcely" means "hardly" or "barely," which means that the speaker knows the person only to a very limited extent, making it unlikely for the speaker to lend the books.

subject: English Language
q: Honeybees have two characteristics similar to humans.
options: they have both male and female sexes
they have a caste system and work for a living
they are social and communicate with one another
explanation: The passage mentions that honeybees are social insects and live together in hives. Additionally, it highlights that they communicate with each other through a unique dancing movement to share information about food sources. This reflects a social structure and communication, which is a characteristic similar to humans.

subject: English Language
q: According to the passage, it is not all honeybees that produce
explanation:
According to the passage, it is not all honeybees that produce eggs. The passage states that only the queens lay eggs:

"The queens' unfertilised eggs develop into drones, whereas fertilised eggs develop into females, which may be either workers or virgin queens."

subject: English Language
q: Another word for the word destined according to the passage would be
explanation: In the passage, the word "destined" refers to the predetermined fate or purpose of the eggs in the honeybee colony. It mentions that "eggs destined to become queens" are deposited in queen cells, meaning these eggs are predetermined to develop into queens.

subject: English Language
q: Apart from being born royal, a queen can lose its royalty by becoming a worker through
explanation: According to the passage, the fate of a virgin queen is determined by the diet she is fed. If she is fed a diet consisting solely of royal jelly, she develops into a queen. However, if she is not fed this diet, she will develop into a worker instead.

subject: English Language
q: Which of the honeybees does not live long?
explanation: According to the passage, drones (the male honeybees) are present only for about eight weeks in early summer. They live for a shorter period compared to the queens and workers, who have longer lifespans.

subject: English Language
q: Choose the option that best fills the gap labelled 6
explanation: The "title catalogue" is the most logical option as it is used to search for books by their title.

subject: English Language
q: Choose the option that best fills the gap labelled 7
explanation: An "author catalogue" helps locate books by the author's name.

subject: English Language
q: Choose the option that best fills the gap labelled 8
explanation: Books are located on a "shelf" in a library.

subject: English Language
q: Choose the option that best fills the gap labelled 9
explanation: A "call-mark" is used to identify the specific location of a book on a shelf.

subject: English Language
q: Choose the option that best fills the gap labelled 10
explanation: The "subject catalogue" is used to find books on a specific subject, like Mathematics.

subject: English Language
q: Choose the option that best fills the gap labelled 11
explanation: A "call-card" is used to reference the specific location of a book based on its call number.

subject: English Language
q: Choose the option that best fills the gap labelled 12
explanation: "Shelf-guides" are used to help guide individuals to the appropriate shelf where books are located

subject: English Language
q: Choose the option that best fills the gap labelled 13
explanation: The "spines" of books have their titles and identification visible for easy location.

subject: English Language
q: Choose the option that best fills the gap labelled 14
explanation: The "lending desk" is the place where a library assistant checks out books to borrowers.

subject: English Language
q: Choose the option that best fils the gap labelled 15
explanation: Libary books have a "pocket" inside where the book card is kept.

Gap
explanation:
gap - /ɡæp/

explanation:

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

The paper stated that the driver of the lorry was helping the police in their investigation.
options: joining the police in their investigation
being questioned at the police station
helping the police to drive around
helping the police who were injured
explanation:
"The paper stated that the driver of the lorry was helping the police in their investigation" means that the driver is cooperating with the police and providing information to assist their investigation.

When a news report says someone is "helping police with their inquiries" or "helping in their investigation," it is a polite way of saying that the person is being questioned by the police in connection with an incident or crime. It doesn't necessarily mean the person is under arrest, but they are providing information that the police believe is relevant to their investigation.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

It is not in my place to keep late, no matter what happens.
options: I am not used to keeping late
I am a habitual latecomer to any event
I do not get to anywhere later than expected
I do not really care whether I get to an event late or not
explanation: It means that the speaker avoids being late under any circumstances.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

Tayo had to separate himself neck and crop from his old friend.
options: He became scared of his old friend
He became suspicious of his old friend
He completely severed from his old friend
He completely forgave his old friend's offences
explanation: The phrase "separate himself neck and crop" means to sever ties entirely or completely break away.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

One of the prerequisites for success in examination is burning the midnight oil.
options: needs to study late into the night
one should burn all the candles
one needs to study at will
explanation: The phrase "burning the midnight oil" is an idiom that means working or studying late into the night.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The instructions given to him are vague.
explanation: The word vague means unclear or not specific. The opposite of this is clear, which means easy to understand or unambiguous.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Due to old age, mother has become taciturn.
explanation: The word taciturn means someone who is habitually quiet or reserved in speech. The opposite is garrulous, which describes someone who is excessively talkative.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

She remembered how reticent her father had been about his past.
explanation: Reticent means reserved or unwilling to share thoughts or feelings. The opposite, forthcoming, describes someone who is open, communicative, and willing to share information.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The trial of the former governor was stopped and the earlier judgement was declared null and void.
explanation: Null and void means invalid or without legal force. The opposite, credible, suggests validity or trustworthiness.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

Unemployment among school leavers has reached epidemic proportions.
explanation: Epidemic proportions refer to something that has grown to an extensive or widespread level, similar to how an epidemic affects a large population.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

At a crucial time, one should be cautious in taking decisive action.
explanation: The word "decisive" means having the power to make firm decisions or determine an outcome. In the sentence, "taking decisive action" refers to making an important and firm decision at a crucial time. Significant is nearest in meaning because a decisive action is one that has an important impact.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The assistant class teacher was told to stand in for Mrs Chukwudi.
explanation: "Stand in for" means to take someone's place or support them temporarily, which is closest in meaning to "support."

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The board Chairman cherishes punctuality at the meeting.
explanation: "Cherishes" means to value or admire something, so "admires" is the closest in meaning.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, select the option that best completes the gap. 

The touts ____ arrested during the riot.
explanation: The subject "touts" is plural, so the correct form of the verb is "were."

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, select the option that best completes the gap. 

The ripe orange was beyond his ______.
explanation: The phrase "beyond his reach" means that something is too far away or inaccessible to him.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, select the option that best completes the gap. 

The students _____ the planned meeting with the new principal.
explanation: "Boycotted" means to intentionally avoid or refuse to participate in something, typically as an expression of protest. This fits the context of students not participating in a planned meeting with the new principal.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, select the option that best completes the gap. 

Take your share and give them _____
explanation: "Theirs" is the possessive form of "they," indicating something that belongs to them. In this case, it refers to the share belonging to others.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, select the option that best completes the gap. 

The children chased all the _______ away.
explanation: "Geese" is the plural form of "goose," and it is the correct word to complete the sentence.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, select the option that best completes the gap. 

He went ____ the corridor, looking for the principal's office.
explanation:
The sentence should read: "He went along the corridor, looking for the principal's office."

"Along" is used to describe movement in a line or path, such as along a corridor.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, select the option that best completes the gap. 

To be alive after such a serious accident _____ a celebration.
explanation:
The sentence should read: "To be alive after such a serious accident calls for a celebration."

This phrase means that a celebration is needed or appropriate in this situation.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, choose the option that rhymes with the given word. 

Light
explanation:
light - /laɪt/

lure - /lʊə/

hate - /heɪt/

right - /raɪt/

caught -  /kɔːt/

subject: English Language
q:
In the following questions, choose the option that rhymes with the given word:

draughts

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, choose the most appropriate stress pattern from the options. The stressed syllable is written in capital letters. 

immunity
explanation: For multi-syllabic words that end in '-ty', the stress tends to fall on the ante-penultimate syllable (i.e. the third syllable from the last). Therefore, the stress placement would be iMMUnity.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, choose the most appropriate stress pattern from the options. The stressed syllable is written in capital letters. 

radioactive
explanation: Words that end with -ive have their stress on the penultimate syllable (i.e. on the second to the last syllable). Therefore, the stress placement is radioACtive.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, the word in capital letter has the emphatic stress. Choose the option to which the given words relate. 

His reign was noted for PEACE.
options: Was his reign noted for wealth?
Whose house is noted for peace?
Was his reign examined for peace?
Whose reign is noted for peace?
explanation: Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particulate word in a sentence for clarification or emphasis. The question requires you to select the question that incites the response given. The word "Peace" is emphasised in the response, and so you are to look for an option that has a word that is different from "peace". The answer is option A because the emphatic stress on PEACE contrasts with wealth in the question, thereby validating and emphasizing the word PEACE in the response.

subject: English Language
q:
In each of the following questions, the word in capital letter has the emphatic stress. Choose the option to which the given words relate. 

Bala ate a slice of BREAD.
options: Did Bala drink a slice of bread?
Did Bala eat a loaf of bread?
Did Olu eat a slice of bread?
Did Bala eat a slice of cake?
explanation: Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particulate word in a sentence for clarification or emphasis. The question requires you to select the question that incites the response given. The word "bread" is emphasised in the response, and so you are to look for an option that has a word that is different from "bread". The answer is option D because the emphatic stress on BREAD contrasts with cake in the question, thereby validating and emphasizing the word BREAD in the response.

subject: English Language
q: The word anguish as used in the passage means
explanation: In the context of the passage, anguish refers to extreme suffering or pain, both physical and emotional, as described by the harrowing experiences of the people.

subject: English Language
q: The phrase on that fateful day is
explanation: The phrase on that fateful day modifies the verb had pursued by providing information about when the action occurred, making it an adverbial phrase.

subject: English Language
q: From the passage, it is clear that
options: everyone from the writer's village arrived Cameroon alive
everyone from the writer's village arrived the mountains alive
some children were not able to make it to Cameroon
all children made it to Cameroon alive despite the snakebites
explanation: This is evident from the passage where it is stated, "Some of them bitten by snakes died because we were not able to carry them to hospital for treatment."

subject: English Language
q: What does the writer mean by we knew what we were up against?
options: The height of the mountains they were running to
The difficulty of life on the mountains
The distance from the mountains to Cameroon
explanation: The phrase shows the writer's awareness of the dangerous and violent nature of the insurgents pursuing them.

subject: English Language
q: According to the writer, travelling across several labyrinths suggests
options: it was easy for them to locate the paths leading to Cameroon
Cameroon is a straight distance from the mountains
they arrived Cameroon after two days of crossing several rivers
they had a complicated and tortuous journey through the bush
explanation: The phrase "travelling across several labyrinths" suggests a challenging and confusing journey. It shows the difficulty in finding their way to Cameroon.

subject: English Language
q:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

It is highly improbable that it will snow in June.
options: It is highly unlikely that it will snow in June
It is possible that it will snow in June
It will never snow in June
The snowfall will probably be high in June
explanation: The word "improbable" means "unlikely to happen". The other options either exaggerate the sentence or does not convey the information in the sentence at all.

subject: English Language
q:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

He played his wife false by inviting his mistress to the house as his cousin.
options: he brought his cousin to his wife's room
His cousin and his wife were friends
He cheated on his wife with his mistress
He helped his wife with his mistress
explanation: The phrase "played his wife false" means he acted deceitfully toward her, and the fact that he invited his mistress to the house as his cousin implies he was unfaithful to his wife.

subject: English Language
q:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

Driving in Lagos is a difficult task.
options: It is an easy task to drive in Lagos
It is a difficult task driving Lagos
It is a herculean task driving in Lagos
It is not a difficult task to drive in Lagos
explanation: The phrase "a difficult task" implies that driving in Lagos is extremely challenging. A "herculean task"  is a task that requires great strength and effort. It is synonymous with a difficult task.

subject: English Language
q:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

I am given a free hand to run this business.
options: I am allowed to do my own business
I alone can choose my executives
explanation: The phrase "given a free hand" means being granted the autonomy or freedom to make decisions and manage something without interference. Hence, option A correctly explains the conveyed meaning.

subject: English Language
q:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

The government has promised to pay our gratuity, we need to have our fingers crossed.
options: We should be united in purpose
explanation: The phrase "have our fingers crossed" means to remain hopeful or wish for a positive outcome. Therefore, option C best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

She looks very pale.
explanation: "Ruddy" refers to a healthy, red or flushed complexion, which is the opposite of "pale" (a lighter or less healthy complexion). Therefore, option A is the opposite in meaning to "pale."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Before going to bed, make sure you extinguish all the lights.
explanation: "Extinguish" means to turn off or put out. The opposite of turning off the lights (extinguishing) would be to "switch on" the lights. Therefore, option C is the opposite in meaning to "extinguish."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Ebere's indolence contrasts with her sister's.
explanation: "Indolence" refers to a state of laziness or avoidance of work. The opposite of laziness or indolence is "diligence," which means careful and persistent work or effort. Therefore, option C is the opposite in meaning to "indolence."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The roads in major cities are broad.
explanation: The word "broad" refers to something wide or spacious. The opposite of broad, in this context, is "narrow," which refers to something thin or restricted in width. Therefore, option D is the opposite in meaning to "broad."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

He gave an indifferent look at his dismissal.
explanation: "Indifferent" means showing a lack of interest or concern. The opposite of indifference, in this case, would be "concerned," meaning showing care or attention. Therefore, option B is the correct opposite in meaning.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The official has declared the election inconclusive.
explanation: "Inconclusive" means something that does not provide clear results or answers. "Indeterminate" carries a similar meaning, indicating something that is uncertain or undefined, making it the closest in meaning.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

His reckless remark did not go unnoticed.
explanation: A "reckless" remark refers to a statement made without careful thought or consideration and that is spontaneous or careless. "Unguarded" means something that is made without restraint or caution.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The old fire station has obviously become derelict.
explanation: "Derelict" means something that is in a state of disrepair or abandonment, which closely relates to "moribund," meaning something that is in a dying or decaying state. Both terms suggest neglect or decline.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Death is inevitable.
explanation: "Inevitable" means something that cannot be avoided or prevented, which is synonymous with "unavoidable." Both terms suggest that an event or outcome is certain to happen.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

I had to put up my house as _____ for the bank loan.
explanation: When someone offers something of value, like a house, in exchange for a loan, it is referred to as "collateral." This is a common practice where the collateral serves as security for the loan in case the borrower fails to repay it.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

The beggar would be grateful for even a _____ of bread.
explanation: The phrase "even a crust of bread" is a common expression used to suggest that someone would be grateful for even a small, humble portion of bread, such as the crust, which is often considered the least desirable part.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

These properties were ____ sale before the fire gutted them.
explanation: The phrase "up for sale" is commonly used to express that something is available or being offered for sale. In this case, it means the properties were available for sale before the fire destroyed them.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

The ______ of armed robbery has been on the increase.
explanation: "Incidence" refers to the occurrence or frequency of an event, typically used in the context of events like crimes or diseases. Incidence on the other hand refers to a specific event or occurrence often unexpected or of a particular nature. In this sentence, we're discussing the general frequency or occurrence of armed robbery. This makes incidence the correct option in this context.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

She hurried off ______ and came back.
explanation: "Fast" is the correct adverb form to describe the manner in which she hurried. "Fast" itself is both an adjective and an adverb. "Fastly" is not the standard adverb form.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

Justina used to be the best-behaved girl, ____?
explanation: In questions like this, the auxiliary verb used in the tag question should match the auxiliary verb or the verb used in the main sentence. All sentences have an auxiliary verb even though it is not visible. Sentences without visible auxiliary verbs carry the auxiliary verb "did" as in "She did used to be the best-behaved girl". Therefore, the correct tag question is "didn't she."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

It was the _______ of brakes that drew our attention.
explanation: The phrase "screeching of brakes" is a common expression used to describe the high-pitched, loud noise made by brakes when a vehicle is stopped suddenly.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

Everyone knew the woman's _______ before his phone went off.
explanation: The word "itinerary" refers to a planned route or journey that details the travel schedule or events. The other options are misspellings of the word.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

Put Chioma's meal in the _______ pack.
explanation:
A. shiny, new, red lunch

The other should be:

  1. Opinion or observation (shiny)
  2. Age or condition (new)
  3. Colour (red)
    Thus, "shiny, new, red" follows the correct order of adjectives before the noun "lunch."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

I was glad he called my attention ____ the matter.
explanation: The correct preposition after the verb "call attention" is "to." We say "call someone's attention to something," meaning to make them aware of or focus on something.

embodiment

declamation

subject: English Language
q:
The word in capital letters has the emphatic stress. Choose the options to which the given sentence relates. 

The architect did the DESIGN.
options: Was the design done by the engineer?
Did the architect destroy the design?
Did the architect do the draft?
explanation: Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particular word in a sentence or utterance for emphasis, correction or clarification. To test for emphatic stress, the contrasting question should affirm or negate the specific word in capital letters ("DESIGN"). Option D contrasts "design" with "draft," making it the appropriate response to confirm emphasis.

subject: English Language
q:
The word in capital letters has the emphatic stress. Choose the options to which the given sentence relates. 

My only SON speaks like an orator.
options: Does my only son speak like an illiterate?
Does my only daughter speak like an orator?
Does my only son read like an orator?
Does your only son speak like an orator?
explanation: For emphatic stress on "SON," the contrasting question should challenge "son" by substituting it with a different noun (e.g., "daughter"). Option B correctly contrasts "son" with "daughter," confirming the emphasis.

subject: English Language
q: According to the passage, students perform poorly in examinations because
options: they fail to identify the ethics of reading
they are too pre-occupied with social activities
of their inability to imbibe good reading culture
explanation: The passage explicitly mentions that one of the main causes of mass failure in examinations is students' inability to imbibe a good reading culture. This is highlighted as the primary reason learners struggle with comprehension and examination performance.

subject: English Language
q: It is implied in the passage that
options: students who cultivate reading habit easily comprehend the intention of the writer
the understanding of the writer will be strenuous for the students who prepare well enough
students who fail to imbibe a good reading culture will find it easy to know the intention of the writer
a student can easily understand the writer's intention whether he imbibes a good reading culture or not
explanation: The passage emphasises the importance of a good reading culture and its connection to effective comprehension. This implies that students who develop the habit of reading are more likely to grasp the writer's intentions and understand the text better.

subject: English Language
q: From the passage, it is implied that
options: few students see reading as a difficult task
all students see reading as a simple task
some students see reading as a difficult task
most students see reading as a difficult task
explanation: The passage states, "most students see it as a tedious task that has to be performed in order to pass a particular examination." This implies that the majority of students perceive reading as a challenging activity.

subject: English Language
q: Fill the gap labelled 6
explanation: The correct phrase is "impart knowledge," meaning to share or communicate knowledge to others.

subject: English Language
q: FIll the gap labelled 7
explanation: Teachers teach various "subjects," which are the areas of study, like mathematics or literature.

subject: English Language
q: Fill the gap labelled 8
explanation: Teachers "guide" students, helping them navigate through learning and life challenges.

subject: English Language
q: Fill the gap labelled 9
explanation: A teacher is a "stakeholder" in the education system. This means that they have a vested interest in its success.

subject: English Language
q: Fill the gap labelled 10
explanation: "Teaching-learning process" refers to the interactive journey of teaching and learning.

subject: English Language
q: Fill the gap labelled 11
explanation: The phrase "move at the speed of the genius" expresses that teachers have to be adaptable to gifted students.

subject: English Language
q: Fill the gap labelled 12
explanation: Teachers manage the "register," which is a record of attendance.

subject: English Language
q: Fill the gap labelled 13
explanation: Teaching "aids" are materials like visual aids, charts or multimedia tools that facilitate learning.

subject: English Language
q: FIll the gap labelled 14
explanation: Teaching is the profession that "birthed" or gave rise to all other professions.

subject: English Language
q: Fill the gap labelled 15
explanation: Teachers should be given "honour" for their critical role in society.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentences.

Adeola lives a stone's throw away from the church.
options: He lives where stones are thrown to the church
He lives a long distance from the church
He lives not too close to the church
He lives very close to the church
explanation: The idiom "a stone's throw" means a very short distance. In this context, it suggests that Adeola's house is very close to the church.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentences.

Here he comes, speak of the devil.
options: He is scared of seeing the devil
He is the representation of the devil
He appears as he is being discussed
explanation: The phrase "speak of the devil" is an idiom used when someone being talked about unexpectedly shows up. It does not imply anything about the person being the devil or associated with evil.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentences.

Moses won the contest hands down.
explanation: The expression "to win hands down" means to achieve victory effortlessly or with little difficulty. It implies that the win was straightforward and without struggle.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentences.

I decided to back up my friend's argument at the meeting.
explanation: To "back up" someone means to support or endorse their position, statement or argument. In this context, the speaker is expressing agreement with their friend's argument during the meeting.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

All men in public life should be altruistic.
explanation: The word "altruistic" means being selfless or showing a concern for the well-being of others. The opposite of this would be "selfish," which means prioritising one's own needs and interests over others.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Everyone thought the politician was invincible.
explanation: The word "invincible" means unable to be defeated or overcome. The opposite is "surmountable," which means capable of being overcome or defeated.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Jemila's austere reaction kept everyone on their toes.
explanation: The word "austere" refers to something strict, severe, or harsh in manner or attitude. The opposite, "benign," means gentle, kind, or mild in character or approach.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

My grandpa's stories are usually phony in nature.
explanation: The word "phony" means fake or not genuine. The opposite of "phony" would be something genuine or based on fact, which is factual.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Ayo does regular exercise to stretch his mind.
explanation: To "stretch" one's mind means to expand or broaden one's mental capacity. The opposite of this would be to "restrict," which means to limit or constrain.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

She is anxious about her son who has been ill.
explanation: "Anxious" means being worried or unsettled, which is closest in meaning to "perturbed," meaning disturbed or troubled.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Everyone thinks Ojo's decision to be a demagogue is ill-advised.
explanation: A demagogue is someone who seeks to gain power by manipulating people's emotions, prejudices or fears. Therefore, the word manipulator is the closest in meaning, as it also implies someone who controls or influences others in a negative way.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Most celebrities usually have a tinge of dishonour in their life.
explanation: The word tinge refers to a slight trace or hint of something, often used to describe a subtle amount of colour or quality. Tint is a synonym, meaning a slight shade or trace of a particular quality, which fits the context of the sentence.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

What a grotesque attire.
explanation: The word grotesque means something that is unnatural, ugly or disturbing in appearance. Horrid is the nearest in meaning, as it conveys a sense of something unpleasant or repulsive.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

A vulture was hovering over the dead goat.
explanation: The word hovering refers to staying in one place in the air and flying around without moving much.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

My uncle is not sure ____ the strange guests were.
explanation: In this sentence, the word "who" is the correct choice because it is used to refer to people. The sentence is asking about the identity of the strange guests, which are people, so "who" is appropriate.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

If her brother ____ here, he would call the police.
explanation:
The sentence is a hypothetical or unreal condition, indicating that the situation is not true at the moment. In English, for unreal or hypothetical situations in the present or future, we use the past simple tense (e.g., "were") instead of "was" for all subjects, even with singular subjects like "he" or "she". Therefore, "were" is the correct choice here.

This is an example of the second conditional, which is used to talk about hypothetical situations.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

When they started the fighting, the man who tried to ____ was knocked down.
explanation: The verb "intervene" means to get involved in a situation to help or prevent something. In this context, the man who tried to intervene (or stop the fighting) was knocked down.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Why do children _____ adults' conversations?
explanation: The phrase "break into" means to interrupt or enter something, such as a conversation, without permission. It fits the context of the sentence, where children are interrupting adults' conversations.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the gap in the sentence.

Mr Ezeh _______ with some solutions to the issue.
explanation: The phrase "came up" means to think of or devise something.

subject: English Language
q:
Select the option that best completes the gap in the sentence. 

Tests reveal that the winning athlete had taken some performance-enhancing ______.
explanation: The phrase "performance-enhancing drugs" is a common expression used to refer to substances that athletes use to improve their performance. "Drugs" is the general and most appropriate term in this context.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the gap in the sentence. 

How many eggs were _____ by the hen?
explanation: The verb "laid" is the correct past tense form of "lay" (when referring to something like eggs being produced). "Lain" is the past participle of "lie" (as in "to recline"), not "lay."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the gap in the sentence. 

Are those girls friends of _____?
explanation: In this sentence, "yours" is a possessive pronoun, which is used to indicate ownership or relationship.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

The jury gave ____ verdict.
explanation: "Its" is the correct possessive form of the pronoun "it," used to show ownership or association. In this case, the verdict belongs to the jury, so "its" is appropriate. "His" and "her" are possessive pronouns used for male and female subjects, respectively, but the jury is a group and takes the neutral "its."

Mother - /ˈmʌð.ə/

moth
explanation:
moth - /mɒθ/

both - /bəʊθ/

what - /wɒt/

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the appropriate stress from the options. The stressed syllable is written in capital letters.

dedication
explanation: For polysyllabic words that end with -ion, the stress tends to be placed on the penultimate syllable.

subject: English Language
q:
The word in capital letters has the emphatic stress. Choose the option to which the given stress relates. 

We WALKED to the school.
options: Did we walk to the stadium?
Did we run to the school?
Did they walk to the school?
Did we walk to the church?
explanation: Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particular word in an utterance. This is usually done for clarification or emphasis. The emphasis on "WALKED" in the sentence suggests that the focus is on the action of walking, implying that it is being contrasted with another action, such as running. Therefore, the question "Did we run to the school?" is a logical counterpart, as it contrasts running with walking.

subject: English Language
q:
The word in capital letters has the emphatic stress. Choose the option to which the given stress relates. 

My mother is expected to be back by this time TOMORROW.
options: Who is expected back by this time tomorrow?
Is my mother expected back by this time next month?
Is my mother assumed back by this time tomorrow?
Is my mother expected gone by this time tomorrow?
explanation: Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particular word in an utterance. This is done for clarification or emphasis. The emphasis on "TOMORROW" in the sentence highlights the specific time frame being discussed. The contrast implied by the emphasis is between "tomorrow" and another time frame, such as "next month." This makes option B the most logical choice, as it contrasts "tomorrow" with "next month."

subject: English Language
q: Select the option that fills the gap labelled 6
explanation: The sentence discusses how monolingual standard languages are seen as the norm and anything differing from this is considered aberrant. "Deviations" fits as it means variations or departures from the standard.

subject: English Language
q: Select the option that fills the gap labelled 7
explanation: A "repertoire" refers to the set of linguistic resources an individual uses.

subject: English Language
q: Select the option that best fills the gap labelled 8
explanation: To conjure means to mentally create or visualise a map.

subject: English Language
q: Select the option that best fills the gap labelled 9
explanation: The sentence refers to the depiction of language distribution on maps. "Coded" fits as it describes how regions are marked or categorized for specific languages.

subject: English Language
q: Select the option that best fills the gap labelled 10
explanation: "Pockets" is commonly used to describe small, isolated groups or areas.

subject: English Language
q: Select the option that best fills the gap labelled 11
explanation: This refers to maps that reflect the dominant language(s) of a nation.

subject: English Language
q: Select the option that best fills the gap labelled 12
explanation: Language maps not only show distributions but also shape discussions and ideologies about language use.

subject: English Language
q: Select the option that best fills the gap labelled 13
explanation: The maps establish a connection between language and territory.

subject: English Language
q: Select the option that best fills the gap labelled 14
explanation: The "territorial principle" is a foundational concept in discussions about linguistic justice, as it establishes rules or standards about territory and language.

subject: English Language
q: Select the option that best fills the gap labelled 15
explanation: The territorial principle "supports" or "forms the basis of" linguistic legislation. "Undergirds" conveys this meaning.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

She is travelling to Abuja. She'll return next week.
options: She is travelling to Abuja to return next week
She is travelling to Abuja but returning next week
She is travelling to Abuja to returning next week
She is travelling to Abuja and returning next week
explanation:
The sentence "She is travelling to Abuja. She'll return next week." conveys two separate actions:

  1. She is currently travelling to Abuja.
  2. She plans to return next week.

Option D combines both ideas in a grammatically correct way using "and" to join the two clauses, accurately reflecting the meaning.

subject: English Language
q:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence

Our school field is bigger than yours.
options: Your school is bigger than ours
Your school is not bigger than ours
Our school field is as big as yours
Your school field is not as big as ours
explanation: The sentence "Our school field is better than yours" implies a comparison in quality, size or condition between the two fields, with the speaker's school field being superior. The phrase "not as big as ours" accurately captures the intended comparison.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence

The manager told his workers to call it a day.
options: They were told to continue working
They were told to intensify their efforts
They were told to stop working
They were told to pause for a few minutes
explanation: The phrase "call it a day" is an idiom that means to stop working for the rest of the day or to end an activity. In this context, the manager instructed the workers to conclude their work for the day.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that explains the information conveyed in the sentence

The teacher believed her students were equal to the task.
options: The students were young and weak
The students were hardworking and dedicated
The students were not adequately prepared
The task was very difficult to carry out
explanation: The phrase "equal to the task" means being capable, competent or well-prepared to handle a challenge. This implies that the teacher had confidence in the students' abilities.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

There is no likeness between him and his brother.
explanation: The word "likeness" means similarity or resemblance. The opposite of this is dissimilarity

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Where is the wealth the rich man accumulated in his lifetime?
explanation: The word "accumulated" means to gather or collect over time. The opposite is "squandered," which means to waste or spend recklessly, leading to a loss instead of accumulation.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The cleaner admires the girls for their neatness.
explanation: The word "admires" means to respect or hold someone in high regard. The opposite is "scorns," which means to show contempt or disdain.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that is opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The commissioner thought that the proposal would fall through.
explanation: The phrase "fall through" means to fail or not materialise. The opposite of "fail" is "succeed."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

His persistently poor performance is already driving his parents round the bend.
options: driving his parents into a corner
explanation: The phrase "driving someone round the bend" is an idiomatic expression that means to cause frustration or distress. While "mad" (option B) might seem like a close option, "causing his parents some problems" is the most accurate choice in this context, as it suggests that the poor performance is creating difficulty for the parents.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

We had to prevail upon him to accept that offer.
explanation: The phrase "prevail upon" means to persuade or convince someone to do something. Therefore, persuade is the nearest in meaning to "prevail upon."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Mr Jebu displayed a high sense of conviviality during the Gala.
explanation: The word "conviviality" refers to a cheerful, friendly and sociable atmosphere. Therefore, friendliness is the nearest in meaning to "conviviality."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Despite the cancellation of the excursion, the students still waited at the school gate.
explanation: The word "cancellation" in this context refers to the ending or termination of an event, and "abortion" can be used to mean the act of stopping something before it is completed.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Accountants are discreet with their records.
explanation: The word "discreet" means showing careful judgment, especially in maintaining privacy or confidentiality. Cautious is the nearest in meaning, as it implies being careful and prudent, similar to being discreet.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the gap(s)

Fierce dogs should be kept in the _______ during the day.
explanation: Dogs are usually kept in kennels.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the gap(s)

We have known Rose for some years now, she is a friend of ____
explanation: This is the correct form of the possessive pronoun and indicates something that belongs to "us." Possessive pronouns do not require an apostrophe.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the gap(s)

The new national security committee ____ retired military personnel.
explanation: The verb comprises means "includes" or "consists of" and does not require the preposition "of."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the gap(s)

His car ____ of fuel so he arrived late for the meeting.
explanation: The phrase ran out is correctly used to show that his car was depleted of fuel, which caused the delay. Using "ran out of" would lead to the repetition of the preposition "of".

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best complete(s) the gap.

________ the word in the Thesaurus!
explanation: The phrase look up is the appropriate phrasal verb used when searching for information in a reference source, such as a Thesaurus. The phrase "look for" is also appropriate as it means to search for something physically or mentally. However, the phrasal verb "look up" is more appropriate for this context.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the gap(s)

Ade was told to finish the assignment ____ time.
explanation: The phrase on time means completing something at or before the expected or scheduled time.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that best completes the gap(s)

_________ is expanding rapidly.
explanation: "The city of Abuja" is the correct phrase because it uses the definite article "The," which is necessary when referring to a specific, known city.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the option that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined.

buoy
explanation:
buoy - /bɔɪ/

boy - /bɔɪ/

trough - /trɒf/

route -  /ruːt/

good - /ɡʊd/

q:
Choose the option that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the letter(s) underlined. 

money
explanation:
money - /ˈmʌn.i/

Monday -  /ˈmʌn.deɪ/

go -  /ɡəʊ/

explanation:

subject: English Language
q:
Choose the most appropriate stress pattern from the options. The stressed syllables are written in capital letters. 

Informative
explanation: For polysyllabic words that end in -ive, the stress is usually placed on the antepenultimate syllable. Therefore, in the word "informative", the stress should be placed on "for".

subject: English Language
q:
The word in capital letters has the emphatic stress. Choose the option to which the given sentence relates.

The PRODUCT was rejected as fake.
options: Was the product rejected though genuine?
Was the produce rejected as fake?
explanation: Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particular word or group of words in an utterance. This is usually done for emphasis or clarification. The question requires you to find the question that relates to the sentence given. The sentence is meant to clarify an error that was made in this question. The emphasised word, therefore, should be different in the question. This is why the answer is option D. "Was the produce rejected as fake?" No, "The PRODUCT was rejected as fake."

subject: English Language
q: What was the common ritual at the morning assembly at Stardom on Tuesdays and Thusdays?
options: The second stanza of the national anthem.
explanation: On Tuesdays and Thursdays, Stardom Schools recited the second stanza of the national anthem: "Oh God of creation, direct our noble cause..." This can be found on page 6 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: How much was the boarding house fee per session at Stardom before it was reduced?
options: one hundred and sixty three thousand naira
two hundred and fifty thousand naira.
one hundred and sixty thousand naira.
explanation: The boarding fee per session was N250,000 before it was reduced to N165,000. This information can be found on page 6 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Who instructed the Chemistry teacher to conclude the assembly after Mr Bepo burst into tears?
explanation: The VP, Mrs Apeh, instructed the Chemistry teacher, Mr Justus Anabel, to conclude the national assembly. This can be found on page 5 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Why did the Vice Principal contact the MD after Mr Bepo’s crying incident at the assembly?
options: Because parents were calling with concerns.
explanation: The VP's phone rang incessantly as she tried to console Mr Bepo. THe calls were from worried parents who had heard different rumours about the incident. Mrs Apeh, at that point, realised the best she could do was to call the MD. This can be found on page 6 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: The principal was nicknamed "The Lekki Headmaster" because...
options: He lived in Lekki during his time as a headmaster.
He humorously imitated characters from the Village Headmaster.
He introduced a new curriculum in the school.
explanation: Mr Bepo was nicknamed The Lekki Headmaster because of the way he used to imitate characters in the old TV drama, Village Headmaster, when he was Headmaster at Stardom Kiddies. This can be found on page 8 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Which teachers were reprimanded because two of their candidates had Ds in their subjects?
options: Mr. Ope Wande and Mrs. Grace Apeh.
Mr. Audu and Mr. Justus Anabel.
Mr. Obong Ukake and Miss Taye Kareem.
Mr. Bepo and Mr. Ope Wande.
explanation: The only teachers reprimanded because two candidates had Ds in their subjects were Mr. Obong Ukaku ad Miss Taye Kareem. These teachers taught Chemistry and Geography respectively. This can be found on page 8 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Who among the staff at Stardom School is also a pastor?
explanation: Mr Ope Wande, the Physics teacher, is also a pastor. This can be found on page 9 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: What was given as a reward to teachers whose students scored distinctions in their subjects?
explanation: The teachers who handled subjects where candidates scored distinctions took home 30,000 each. This can be found on page 8 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Why did the MD decide to send the principal home after the crying incident?
options: To avoid disturbing the students further.
To allow the principal to rest.
explanation: The MD instructed Mr Bepo to be taken home because she felt pressed to move the drama away from the school as soon as possible. She was worried about what the parents would think if they learned that the principal was weeping like a child. This can be found on page 9 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Who accompanied the principal to his home on the day of the crying incident?
explanation: The guidance counsellor accompanied Mr Bepo home on the day of the crying incident, as instructed by the MD. This can be found on page 9 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: What was the student Ikenna Egbu’s speech about?
options: The “Japa” trend in our modern society.
The principal’s contributions to the school.
explanation: Ikenna's speech is about his recent excursion to Jos. He described Jos as a brilliantly beautiful city. This can be found on page 7 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Why is Mr. Bepo reluctant to relocate to the United Kingdom?
options: He does not want to leave his students.
He is afraid of adapting to a new environment.
He believes life in Nigeria is better.
He does not want to leave his wife and children behind.
explanation: Mr Bepo is reluctant to relocate to the United Kingdom because he very much loved Stardom Schools, especially is students. He cared about them and wanted to continually be part of their growth. This ca be found on page 10 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: What trait earned Mr. Bepo the nickname “The Lekki Headmaster”?
options: His ability to resolve conflicts amicably
explanation: Mr Audu joked about Bepo's disposition saying: "The way the headmaster masterly resolves these fights reminds me of the way King Oloja, in Village Headmaster, resolved all the roforofos brought by his chiefs and villages. So, as they had their own Village Headmaster, we also have our own Lekki Headmaster." This can be found on page 10 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Who coined the nickname “The Lekki Headmaster”?
explanation: Mr Audu coined the name by comparing him to King Oloja from Village Headmaster. This can be found on page 10 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: How much does Seri earn monthly as a nurse in the United Kingdom?
explanation: His wife, Seri, was a nurse who, according to rumours, was earning up to 10,000 pounds per month. This can be found on page 11 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: What was Mr. Bepo’s monthly salary at Stardom Schools?
explanation: As a principal, Mr Bepo's salary was about N400,000. This can be found on page 11 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Why do Mr. Bepo’s colleagues find his reluctance to relocate amusing?
options: They think he is afraid of flying.
They assume he wants to stay in Nigeria to fulfil his entrepreneurial dreams.
They think he dislikes the United Kingdom.
They believe he should jump at the opportunity to earn more abroad.
explanation: The staff at Stardom Schools find his reluctance to relocate amusing because they believe he should jump at the opportunity to earn abroad. They did the comparison of the amount earned both in Nigeria and abroad and resolved that it would be more profitable to relocate abroad. This can be found on page 11 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Why did the Fruitful Future school fail?
options: Bepo was not willing to be involved in the ritual practices in the area
explanation: Fruitful Future school failed because the roads around the school began to depreciate due to government negligence, as a result, most of the parents started to relocate. The parents that were left could not afford the school fees This can be found on page 39 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Which factor makes Mr. Bepo cautious about entering the commercial transportation business?
options: Lack of interest in the sector
explanation: Mr Bepo was wary about entering the commercial transportation business venture because the drivers were untrustworthy. They either frustrated the idea, defaulted on payment, or colluded with mechanics to defraud the vehicle owners. This can be found on page 12 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: What does the term roforofos that Mr Audu used when talking about Mr Bepo most likely refer to?
explanation: The term roforofos as used by Mr Audu on page 10 of the novel refers to "petty disagreements and conflicts". These were the conflicts that King Oloja, in Village Headmaster, tried to resolve.

subject: English Language
q: What does Mr. Bepo’s reluctance to relocate reveal about his values?
options: He values financial security over personal fulfilment.
He prioritises his students and professional relationships.
He feels unprepared for a new environment.
explanation: The fact that Mr Bepo was reluctant to relocate abroad despite the financial, familial and professional benefits that he would obtain if he did so shows that he valued his students and their growth over all else.

subject: English Language
q: According to Bepo, why is the hourly payment system beneficial?
options: It allows employees to earn more.
It enables employees to change jobs easily
It reduces the workload for employees.
It allows employees to take more holidays.
explanation: Bepo believes that the hourly system of payment is much more beneficial to the employees than the monthly system.  This is because it would make hob schedules flexible and offer the employee an opportunity to change jobs as often as he could. This can be found on page14 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: According to the novel, what is the approximate annual migration rate of Nigerian doctors?
explanation: According to the novel, up to 1000 out of the country's 3000 doctors were migrating annually. This can be found on page 15 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: What unethical act did Mr. Nku engage in before relocating abroad?
options: He borrowed N2 million from the school’s cooperative
He falsified documents to gain a visa.
He stole money from the school’s treasury.
explanation: Mr Nku, one of the staff at Stardom Schools, took a N2 million loan from the school's cooperative and relocated abroad two days later. This can be found on page 15 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Why did one of the school drivers attempt to sell the school bus?
options: To pay his son’s tuition fees abroad
To finance a trip to the UK
explanation: One of the school's drivers attempted to steal the bus in his custody because he wanted to sell it and sue the proceeds to send his son to college abroad. This can be found on page 15 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: According to the novel, why do most Nigerians prefer relocating to the UK?
options: Free education and healthcare for their children
Higher job opportunities compared to other countries
explanation: Accordig to the novel, People in the UK understand that there is an opportunity for their children to enjoy quality education and the healthcare system is much better than in Nigeria. This can be found on page 16 and 17 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: What proverb does Bepo recall from his Idoma co-tenant?
options: “Whether the employer records gains or not, the employee will yet take home his full pay.”
“Life’s intriguing tests yield the same results for everyone.”
“The sugarcane and the bitter leaf get different tastes from the same rain.”
explanation: Bepo remembers this proverb when he realises that people always have different results when they relocate abroad. This proverb can be found on page 17 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: What lesson does Hope’s story about relocating to the UK teach?
options: Financial independence is critical when relocating.
Couples always succeed when they share responsibilities.
Relocation is a foolproof plan for success.
explanation: Hope was an accountant who applied for and secured admission to a Master's in the UK. His wife joined him to work and earn the money that would see him through. But barely four months later, his wife stopped providing for him. She explained that the demands of her job made it impracticable to keep sponsoring him. This teaches the importance of financial independence when relocating abroad This can be found on page 17 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: How much did Sola and her husband borrow to fund their relocation?
explanation: Sola and her husband had to borrow about N4 million to fund their relocation. This can be found on page 16 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Where does Bepo live?
explanation: Bepo lives at Adeniyi Jones, Ikeja, Lagos. This can be found on page 19 of the novel.

subject: English Language
q: Why does Mr Ignatius want to relocate with his family?
options: To secure a better future for his children.
To reunite with his extended family abroad.
To enrol his children in Stardom schools abroad.
